evals.name: simple-form-submission
evals.prompt: I need to test a contact form on my website. The form has a Name field, Email field, and a Submit button. Users should be able to fill in their details and submit the form. Can you help me create a comprehensive Playwright test with page objects and fixtures for this?
evals.expected_output: A complete test suite with: (1) Page object class with goto(), fillForm(), and submit() methods, (2) Spec file with test cases, (3) Fixture file with page object injection, (4) All code should be syntactically valid TypeScript

evals.name: multi-step-workflow
evals.prompt: I want to automate testing for a shopping cart flow: 1) Navigate to product page, 2) Select a product, 3) Add to cart, 4) Go to checkout, 5) Fill shipping info, 6) Confirm order. The steps span multiple pages. Please create Playwright tests that cover this entire workflow with proper page objects.
evals.expected_output: Multiple page objects (ProductPage, CartPage, CheckoutPage) with clear methods, test spec file demonstrating the workflow, fixture file combining all pages, proper error handling and assertions

evals.name: edge-case-validation
evals.prompt: I have a login form and I want to test edge cases: empty fields, invalid email format, password too short, special characters in username. How should I structure these tests? I want page objects, test fixtures, and clear test organization.
evals.expected_output: Multiple test cases covering edge cases, page object with reusable methods for validation, fixtures properly set up, assertions checking error messages, proper test naming conventions

evals.name: unclear-triggering-scenario
evals.prompt: Can you write me a script that checks if a website is down?
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT be triggered or should redirect to appropriate tool. Skill is for Playwright test generation, not website monitoring.
